description: Corrected multiverse reporting with within-class and cross-class statistics separated

cross_class_statistics.note: Cross-class comparison mixes model types with different importance scales, inflating non-replicability and degrading R2

honest_framing.headline: 95% of feature importance claims are non-replicable across the FULL multiverse (3 model classes x 3 scalers x 3 feature selections). Within model classes, non-replicability is lower (75% for XGBoost and RF, 100% for Ridge) but still high.
honest_framing.gaussian_formula: The Gaussian flip formula achieves Spearman rho = 0.78 across the full multiverse (correctly ranking which comparisons are most unreliable) but R2 = -0.39 for predicted flip rates (the absolute predictions fail when mixing model classes with different importance scales). Within model classes, the formula performs better: Ridge R2 = 0.49, XGBoost R2 = 0.33.
honest_framing.caveat: The 95% non-replicable figure is the most dramatic but also the least meaningful scientifically, as it compares tree-based and linear models that measure fundamentally different quantities (gain-based vs coefficient-based importance). The within-class figures (75%) are the more appropriate adversarial baseline.
